publications.ref: Collins MO, Wright JC, Jones M, Rayner JC, Choudhary JS. Confident and sensitive phosphoproteomics using combinations of collision induced dissociation and electron transfer dissociation. J Proteomics. 2014 Mar 21;103C:1-14

Characteristics[Organism].evidence: We analysed the Plasmodium falciparum schizont phosphoproteome

Characteristics[DevelopmentalStage].evidence: We analysed the Plasmodium falciparum schizont phosphoproteome

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No mention of TMT, iTRAQ, or SILAC labeling methods in the text, and the focus is on fragmentation methods (CID/ETD) rather than chemical labeling

Characteristics[Strain].reasoning: PRIDE database indicates isolate 3d7, but this specific strain is not mentioned in the manuscript text
Characteristics[Strain].needs: Methods section confirmation of the specific P. falciparum strain used

Characteristics[MaterialType].reasoning: Phosphoproteomics study requires protein extraction, but specific sample preparation not detailed in abstract
Characteristics[MaterialType].needs: Sample preparation methodology from materials and methods

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a phosphoproteomics study of Plasmodium falciparum schizont stage parasites using label-free quantification. The experiment focuses on comparing CID and ETD fragmentation methods with data-dependent neutral loss triggered ETD strategy. The specific strain (3d7 from PRIDE) and detailed sample preparation methods need confirmation from the full methods section.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: proteins were digested with Trypsin Gold (Promega) for 4 h at 37 °C at an enzyme substrate ratio of 1:20

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: cysteine alkylation was performed in situ with iodoacetamide

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: extraction buffer (4% SDS, 0.1 M DTT, 0.1 M Tris pH 8, 0.5 mM PMSF, 2 μg/ml Aprotinin/Leupeptin, 20 μM ZnCl and 25 mM Sodium fluoride)

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No labeling reagents mentioned in the protocol, consistent with previous confirmation

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: with Acetyl (Protein N-term), Oxidation (M), Deamidated (NQ), Carbamidomethyl (C) and Phospho (ST) set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: with Acetyl (Protein N-term), Oxidation (M), Deamidated (NQ), Carbamidomethyl (C) and Phospho (ST) set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: with Acetyl (Protein N-term), Oxidation (M), Deamidated (NQ), Carbamidomethyl (C) and Phospho (ST) set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: with Acetyl (Protein N-term), Oxidation (M), Deamidated (NQ), Carbamidomethyl (C) and Phospho (ST) set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].4.evidence: with Acetyl (Protein N-term), Oxidation (M), Deamidated (NQ), Carbamidomethyl (C) and Phospho (ST) set as variable modifications

Comment[EnrichmentMethod].evidence: IMAC purifications were performed as described, with the following modifications. Peptides were re-suspended in IMAC loading buffer (50% acetonitrile, 0.1% TFA) and incubated with pre-equilibrated Phos-Select beads (Sigma)

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: Phosphopeptides from two IMAC purifications were pooled and split into 6 aliquots for LC–MS/MS analysis

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: Phosphopeptides from two IMAC purifications were pooled and split into 6 aliquots for LC–MS/MS analysis

Comment[Separation].evidence: then separated using a 320 min RP gradient (4–30% acetonitrile/0.1% formic acid) on an Acclaim PepMap100 C18 analytical column

Characteristics[Modification].5.reasoning: CID and ETD spectra from DT experiments and CID spectra from DDNL experiments were also searched with the addition of Phospho Y to identify putative tyrosine phosphorylation sites
Characteristics[Modification].5.needs: Confirmation whether Phospho Y was used as a standard modification or only for specific searches

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation details are now fully characterized: trypsin digestion with DTT reduction and iodoacetamide alkylation using FASP protocol. Five variable modifications confirmed (Phospho ST, Acetyl N-term, Oxidation M, Deamidated NQ, Carbamidomethyl C). IMAC phosphopeptide enrichment with 6 fractions per sample, analyzed by reverse phase LC-MS/MS. Tyrosine phosphorylation searches were performed but unclear if Phospho Y should be listed as standard modification.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: coupled to an LTQ Orbitrap Velos hybrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: top 10 most abundant multiply-charged (2 + and higher) ions in a given chromatographic window subjected to either CID or ETD fragmentation in the linear ion trap with supplemental activation enabled

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: The mass spectrometer was operated in standard data dependent acquisition mode controlled by Xcalibur 2.1

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: equipped with a nanospray ion source

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: subjected to either CID or ETD fragmentation in the linear ion trap

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: trypsin with a maximum of 3 missed cleavages

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: separated using a 320 min RP gradient (4–30% acetonitrile/0.1% formic acid)

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: with a flow rate of 0.3 μl/min

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: All major LC-MS/MS parameters have been successfully extracted and confirmed. The LTQ Orbitrap Velos used data-dependent acquisition with both CID and ETD fragmentation methods. Key parameters include 10 ppm precursor tolerance, 0.5 Da fragment tolerance, 320 min gradient at 0.3 μl/min flow rate, and maximum 3 missed cleavages for trypsin. Collision energy was not specified in the methods. All instrumentation and acquisition parameters are now fully characterized for SDRF completion.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: Triplicate DT experiments were performed... Triplicate DDNL experiments were performed... triplicate experiments using CID fragmentation and triplicate DT experiments

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: All experiments described as 'triplicate' refer to biological replicates with no mention of technical replicates

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Raw file names contain clear tokens: -01 and -02 indicating fraction numbers. DDNL and DT refer to different acquisition methods (data-dependent neutral loss vs dual fragmentation)

FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].evidence: File names clearly indicate two experimental approaches: IMACDDNL (data-dependent neutral loss) and IMACDT (dual fragmentation), with fraction numbers -01 and -02

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: Six raw files provided, representing different fractions and acquisition methods from triplicate experiments

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].reasoning: Files have different dates (Mar06, Mar09, Mar10, Mar11) suggesting different biological replicates, but cannot definitively assign replicate numbers without clearer naming convention
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].needs: More explicit replicate numbering in filenames or additional metadata

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Successfully identified experimental design with 3 biological replicates and 1 technical replicate per sample. Raw file analysis reveals 6 samples representing two acquisition methods (DDNL and DT) with clear fraction identifiers (-01, -02). The study compares data-dependent neutral loss and dual fragmentation approaches across multiple fractions from IMAC-enriched phosphopeptides. Biological replicate assignment remains unclear due to ambiguous date-based naming rather than explicit replicate numbering.
